title: [BUG] Heap buffer overflow in `cmp-cuda` at (`compute_semblances` kernel)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`cmp-cuda`: running the program with a crafted `.su` seismic file whose `dt` field (sample interval, uint16) is `0` causes a GPU illegal memory access — an out-of-bounds read of the `samples` array inside the `compute_semblances` GPU kernel at `src/cmp-cuda/main.cu:106`.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/cmp-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
compute-sanitizer --tool=memcheck ./main \
  -i cmp-dt0-overflow.txt -c0 1.98e-7 -c1 1.77e-6 -nc 4 -aph 1000 -tau 0.002 -v 0
```

### PoC

[cmp-dt0-overflow.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29723712/cmp-dt0-overflow.txt)

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: 043970e363cc72219182e3be4b72b6adf9d13a3e

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
========= COMPUTE-SANITIZER
========= Invalid __global__ read of size 4 bytes
=========     at compute_semblances(const float *, const float *, const float *, float *, float *, int, int, float, float, int, int, int, int)+0x13e0 in main.cu:106
=========     by thread (0,0,0) in block (0,0,0)
=========     Access to 0x8001cbc009fc is out of bounds
=========     and is 8474593277 bytes after the nearest allocation at 0x7fffd2a00000 of size 512 bytes
=========     Saved host backtrace up to driver entry point at kernel launch time
=========         Host Frame: main [0xc286] in main
```

### Root Cause

> On the tested NVIDIA GPU (A30 / sm_80): `(int)(+Inf) = INT_MAX = 2147483647`, `(int)NaN = 0`, `(int)(-Inf) = INT_MIN`.

```cpp
// main.cu:209-213
dt = dt / 1000000.0f;
real idt = 1.0f / dt; // dt=0 → idt = +Inf
...
// main.cu:97-106
real t = sqrtf(_t0 + _c * h[t_id]) * _idt;  // _idt=+Inf → t=+Inf (any h>0)
int it = (int)( t );                         // (int)Inf = INT_MAX on tested GPU (A30 / sm_80)
int ittau = it - _tau;
...
if(ittau >= 0 && it + _tau + 1 < ns) {      // INT_MAX overflows → −2147483646 < ns=16 → TRUE
    int k1 = ittau + (t_id-t_id0)*ns;        // k1 = INT_MAX + 0 = 2147483647
    ...
    sk1p1 = samples[k1];                     // OOB: samples is 512 bytes
```

### Suggested Fix

```diff
--- a/src/cmp-cuda/main.cu
+++ b/src/cmp-cuda/main.cu
@@ -95,6 +95,7 @@ __global__ void compute_semblances(...)
     for(int t_id=t_id0; t_id < t_idf; t_id++) {
       real t = sqrtf(_t0 + _c * h[t_id]) * _idt;
+      if (!isfinite(t) || t < 0.0f) { err++; continue; }

       int it = (int)( t );
       int ittau = it - _tau;
```

title: [BUG] Null pointer dereference in `bfs-cuda` at (`Kernel` kernel)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`bfs-cuda`: running the program with the crafted graph file `bfs-null-edges.txt` causes a GPU illegal memory access — a null-pointer dereference of the edge array `d_graph_edges` inside the `Kernel` GPU function in `src/bfs-cuda/bfs.cu`.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/bfs-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
compute-sanitizer --tool=memcheck ./main ./bfs-null-edges.txt
```

### PoC

[bfs-null-edges.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29723547/bfs-null-edges.txt)

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: 15ff7a0976d493625a4948c299e04654bcfa4ef9

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
========= Invalid __global__ read of size 4 bytes
=========     at Kernel(const Node *, const int *, char *, char *, const char *, int *, int)+0x240 in bfs.cu:37
=========     by thread (0,0,0) in block (0,0,0)
=========     Access to 0x0 is out of bounds
=========     and is 140736611745792 bytes before the nearest allocation at 0x7fffcbc00000 of size 32 bytes
=========     Saved host backtrace up to driver entry point at kernel launch time
=========         Host Frame: run_bfs_gpu(int, Node*, int, int*, char*, char*, char*, int*) [0xaf5c] in main
=========         Host Frame: main [0x9de0] in main
```

### Root Cause

`run_bfs_gpu()` in `bfs.cu` (in `src/bfs-cuda/bfs.cu:114-115`) allocates and initialises the edge list:

```cpp
int* d_graph_edges;
cudaMalloc((void**) &d_graph_edges, sizeof(int)*edge_list_size);
cudaMemcpy(d_graph_edges, h_graph_edges, sizeof(int)*edge_list_size, ...);
```

When `edge_list_size == 0`, `cudaMalloc(..., 0)` may return `NULL`. Node 0 in the crafted graph has `no_of_edges = 2` (parsed before the corrupt byte stopped `fscanf`), so the `Kernel` is launched with node 0 in the frontier and immediately tries to read `d_graph_edges[0]` with `d_graph_edges == NULL`.

### Suggested Fix

```diff
--- a/src/bfs-cuda/bfs.cu
+++ b/src/bfs-cuda/bfs.cu
@@ -109,6 +109,12 @@ void run_bfs_gpu(int no_of_nodes, Node* h_graph_nodes, int edge_list_size,
 {
+  // Validate adjacency bounds before touching the GPU.
+  for (int i = 0; i < no_of_nodes; i++) {
+    int end = h_graph_nodes[i].starting + h_graph_nodes[i].no_of_edges;
+    if (h_graph_nodes[i].starting < 0 || end < 0 || end > edge_list_size)
+      return;
+  }
+
   char *d_graph_mask;
   cudaMalloc((void**) &d_graph_mask, sizeof(char)*no_of_nodes);
```

title: [BUG] Heap buffer underflow in `mf-sgd-cuda` at (`grid_problem()` function)
author: Mingkai-Li

title: [BUG] Invalid memory access in `gd-cuda` at (`get_CRSM_from_svm()` function)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`gd-cuda`: running the program with the crafted LibSVM file `gd-empty-line.txt` causes a host-side out-of-bounds dereference (SIGSEGV) in `get_CRSM_from_svm()` in `src/gd-cuda/utils.cpp`.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/gd-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
./main gd-empty-line.txt 0.0001 1 1
```

### PoC

> The file is a valid LibSVM file with 40 normal data lines followed by one empty line at line 39.

[gd-empty-line.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29722671/gd-empty-line.txt)

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: 15ff7a0976d493625a4948c299e04654bcfa4ef9

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
Processing the SVM file
[1]    673455 segmentation fault  ./main  0.0001 1 1
```

### Root Cause

`get_CRSM_from_svm()` in `utils.cpp` (in `src/gd-cuda/utils.cpp:35`) reads the LibSVM file line-by-line with `std::getline`, splits each line on whitespace, then accesses `splitString[0]` without first checking whether `splitString` is empty:

```cpp
// utils.cpp:30-35
while (getline(libsvm_file, observation)) {
    std::istringstream iss_obs(observation);
    std::vector<std::string> splitString(
        std::istream_iterator<std::string>{iss_obs},
        std::istream_iterator<std::string>());
    M.y_label.push_back(std::stoi(splitString[0]));  // utils.cpp:35 - UB if splitString is empty
```

When the parser encounters a blank line, `splitString` is empty and `splitString[0]` is an out-of-bounds `std::vector` access.

### Suggested Fix

```diff
--- a/src/gd-cuda/utils.cpp
+++ b/src/gd-cuda/utils.cpp
@@ -30,6 +30,7 @@ void get_CRSM_from_svm(Classification_Data_CRS &M, const std::string &file_path
       std::vector<std::string> splitString(std::istream_iterator<std::string>{iss_obs},
                                            std::istream_iterator<std::string>());
+      if (splitString.empty()) continue;
       M.y_label.push_back(std::stoi(splitString[0]));
```

title: [BUG] Heap buffer overflow in `sobel-cuda` at (`SDKBitMap::load()` function)
author: Mingkai-Li

title: [BUG] Invalid free in `bitcracker-cuda` at (`parse_data` function)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`bitcracker-cuda`: running the program with the crafted BitLocker hash file `bitcracker-invalid-free.txt` causes a host-side crash (SIGSEGV / heap corruption) in `parse_data()` in `src/bitcracker-cuda/utils.cu`. The `parse_data` error path calls `free(hash)` on a pointer that has been advanced past the start of its malloc'd allocation, corrupting the heap.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/bitcracker-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
./main -f ./bitcracker-invalid-free.txt -d ./wordlist.txt
```

### PoC

[bitcracker-invalid-free.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29721904/bitcracker-invalid-free.txt)

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: 15ff7a0976d493625a4948c299e04654bcfa4ef9

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
---------> BitCracker: BitLocker password cracking tool <---------


==================================
Retrieving Info
==================================

Reading hash file "bitcracker-invalid-free.txt"
Wrong Salt size
[1]    653722 segmentation fault  ./main -f  -d ./wordlist.txt
```

### Root Cause

`parse_data()` in `utils.cu` allocates a buffer for the hash line:

```cpp
hash = (char *) Calloc(INPUT_HASH_SIZE, sizeof(char)); // utils.cu:87

if(fgets(hash, INPUT_HASH_SIZE, fphash) == NULL)
{
  fprintf(stderr, "No correct input hash provided\n");
  goto out;
}
...
hash += HASH_TAG_LEN;      // utils.cu:104 - advances the pointer
```

After advancing `hash` by `HASH_TAG_LEN` bytes, every error path that jumps to the `out:` label calls:

```cpp
free(hash);   // utils.cu:196 - BUG: frees the interior of the allocation, not the base pointer
```

### Suggested Fix

```diff
--- a/src/bitcracker-cuda/utils.cu
+++ b/src/bitcracker-cuda/utils.cu
@@ -82,7 +82,7 @@ int parse_data(...)
-  hash = (char *) Calloc(INPUT_HASH_SIZE, sizeof(char));
+  char *hash_base = (char *) Calloc(INPUT_HASH_SIZE, sizeof(char));
+  hash = hash_base;
   ...
   hash += HASH_TAG_LEN;
   ...
 out:
   fclose(fphash);
   free(*salt); free(*nonce); free(*vmk); free(*mac);
-  free(hash);
+  free(hash_base);
```

title: [bitcracker] Missing input data
author: maarquitos14
body:
https://github.com/ORNL/HeCBench/blob/5cf70db98e1d2f82aaebc6ae97b81c1000175faf/src/bitcracker-hip/Makefile#L54-L57

`run` target for bitcracker-hip needs `img_win8_user_hash.txt` and `user_passwords_60000.txt`, but `bitcracker-cuda/hash_pass` only contains `password_generator.py`.

Is it possible to either upload the files or get instructions on how to generate them?

title: [BUG] Heap buffer overflow in `grep-cuda` at (`ppost2nfa` kernel)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`grep-cuda`: A heap buffer overflow occurs in the `ppost2nfa` kernel when running the `grep-cuda` program.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/grep-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
printf 'a\n' > grep-poc.txt
compute-sanitizer --tool memcheck ./main -f grep-poc.txt "$(printf '\x1b')"
```

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: 09668a47831f7f552c0f2223f5088f8c4acacb73

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
========= Invalid __local__ read of size 8 bytes
=========     at ppost2nfa(char *, State *, int *, State *)+0x1db0 in putil.cu:111
=========     by thread (0,0,0) in block (0,0,0)
=========     Access to 0xffb728 is out of bounds
=========         Device Frame: parallelMatch(char *, const unsigned int *, int, char *, const unsigned int *, unsigned char *, State *)+0x14c0 in pnfa.cu:226
=========     Saved host backtrace up to driver entry point at kernel launch time
=========         Host Frame: main [0x9fec] in main
=========
```

### Root Cause

```cpp
      case CONCATENATE:  /* catenate */
        e2 = pop();
        e1 = pop(); // putil.cu:111
        ppatch(e1.out, e2.start);
        push(pfrag(e1.start, e2.out));
        break;
```

### Suggested Fix

```diff
--- a/src/grep-cuda/putil.cu
+++ b/src/grep-cuda/putil.cu
@@ -92,8 +92,8 @@ ppost2nfa(char *postfix, State *lstate, int *pnstate, State *pmatchstate)
   if(postfix == NULL)
     return NULL;

-#define push(s) *stackp++ = s
-#define pop() *--stackp
+#define push(s) do { if (stackp >= stack + 1000) return NULL; *stackp++ = (s); } while (0)
+#define pop()   (stackp <= stack ? (Frag){NULL, NULL} : *--stackp)
```

title: [BUG] Heap buffer overflow in `perlin-cuda` at (`noiseAt` kernel)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`perlin-cuda`: A heap buffer overflow occurs in the `noiseAt` kernel when running the `perlin-cuda` program.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/perlin-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
compute-sanitizer --tool=memcheck ./main
```

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: 09668a47831f7f552c0f2223f5088f8c4acacb73

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
========= Invalid __global__ read of size 4 bytes
=========     at noiseAt(float, float, int)+0xd30 in noise.cu:84
=========     by thread (14,9,0) in block (546,19,0)
=========     Access to 0x7ffdac4004ec is out of bounds
=========     and is 20 bytes before the nearest allocation at 0x7ffdac400500 of size 32 bytes
=========         Device Frame: sumOctaves(float, float, NoiseParams)+0x9c0 in noise.cu:115
=========         Device Frame: perlin(int, int, NoiseParams, unsigned char *)+0x2f0 in noise.cu:127
=========     Saved host backtrace up to driver entry point at kernel launch time
=========         Host Frame: Perlin::calculate(unsigned char*, NoiseParams, CUstream_st**, int) [0xab82] in main
=========         Host Frame: main [0x9cab] in main
=========
========= Invalid __global__ read of size 4 bytes
=========     at noiseAt(float, float, int)+0xd60 in noise.cu:85
=========     by thread (0,14,0) in block (545,19,0)
=========     Access to 0x7ffdac4004ec is out of bounds
=========     and is 20 bytes before the nearest allocation at 0x7ffdac400500 of size 32 bytes
=========         Device Frame: sumOctaves(float, float, NoiseParams)+0x9c0 in noise.cu:115
=========         Device Frame: perlin(int, int, NoiseParams, unsigned char *)+0x2f0 in noise.cu:127
=========     Saved host backtrace up to driver entry point at kernel launch time
=========         Host Frame: Perlin::calculate(unsigned char*, NoiseParams, CUstream_st**, int) [0xab82] in main
=========         Host Frame: main [0x9cab] in main
```

### Root Cause

```cpp
  const int ix0 = ix & 255, iy0 = iy & 255;
  const int ix1 = (ix0 + 1) & 255, iy1 = (iy0 + 1) & 255;
  const int h0 = _hash[ix0], h1 = _hash[ix1];
  const int iTL = (_hash[h0 + iy0] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS,
            iTR = (_hash[h1 + iy0] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS,
            iBL = (_hash[h0 + iy1] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS,
            iBR = (_hash[h1 + iy1] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS;
  const float2 gTopLeft  = make_float2(gradientX[iTL], gradientY[iTL]);
  const float2 gTopRight = make_float2(gradientX[iTR], gradientY[iTR]);
  const float2 gBotLeft  = make_float2(gradientX[iBL], gradientY[iBL]); // noise.cu:84
  const float2 gBotRight = make_float2(gradientX[iBR], gradientY[iBR]); // noise.cu:85
```

When calculating `iTL`, `iTR`, `iBL`, and `iBR`, Expressions like `_hash[h0 + iy0]` can index beyond the 256-element `_hash` array because `h0`/`h1` and `iy0`/`iy1` are each in `[0, 255]`, so `h0 + iy0` can reach 510.

### Suggested Fix

```diff
--- a/src/perlin-cuda/noise.cu
+++ b/src/perlin-cuda/noise.cu
@@ -75,10 +75,10 @@ __device__ float noiseAt(float x, float y, int seed) {
   const int ix0 = ix & 255, iy0 = iy & 255;
   const int ix1 = (ix0 + 1) & 255, iy1 = (iy0 + 1) & 255;
   const int h0 = _hash[ix0], h1 = _hash[ix1];
-  const int iTL = (_hash[h0 + iy0] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS,
-            iTR = (_hash[h1 + iy0] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS,
-            iBL = (_hash[h0 + iy1] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS,
-            iBR = (_hash[h1 + iy1] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS;
+  const int iTL = (_hash[(h0 + iy0) & 255] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS,
+            iTR = (_hash[(h1 + iy0) & 255] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS,
+            iBL = (_hash[(h0 + iy1) & 255] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS,
+            iBR = (_hash[(h1 + iy1) & 255] + seed) % N_GRADIENTS;
   const float2 gTopLeft  = make_float2(gradientX[iTL], gradientY[iTL]);
   const float2 gTopRight = make_float2(gradientX[iTR], gradientY[iTR]);
   const float2 gBotLeft  = make_float2(gradientX[iBL], gradientY[iBL]);
```

title: [BUG] Heap buffer overflow in `histogram-cuda` at (`histogram_gmem_accum` kernel)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`histogram-cuda`: A heap buffer overflow occurs in the `histogram_gmem_accum` kernel when running the `histogram-cuda` program.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/histogram-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
compute-sanitizer --tool=memcheck ./main --i=1
```

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: 09668a47831f7f552c0f2223f5088f8c4acacb73

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
========= Invalid __global__ write of size 4 bytes
=========     at void histogram_gmem_accum<(int)1024, (int)1, (int)256>(const unsigned int *, int, unsigned int *)+0x320 in histogram_gmem_atomics.h:95
=========     by thread (0,0,0) in block (2,0,0)
=========     Access to 0x7fffc9dfa800 is out of bounds
=========     and is 1 bytes after the nearest allocation at 0x7fffc9dfa400 of size 1024 bytes
=========     Saved host backtrace up to driver entry point at kernel launch time
=========         Host Frame: cudaLaunchKernel [0x87987] in main
=========         Host Frame: double run_gmem_atomics<1, 256, uchar1>(uchar1*, int, int, unsigned int*, bool) [0xfc9c] in main
=========         Host Frame: void RunTest<1, 256, uchar1>(std::vector<std::pair<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> >, double>, std::allocator<std::pair<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> >, double> > >&, uchar1*, int, int, unsigned int*, unsigned int*, int, char const*, char const*, double (*)(uchar1*, int, int, unsigned int*, bool)) [0x147bc] in main
=========         Host Frame: void TestMethods<1, 1, 256, uchar1>(uchar1*, int, int, int, double) [0x14f28] in main
=========         Host Frame: TestGenres(uchar4*, int, int, int, double) [0xeff1] in main
=========         Host Frame: main [0xa5a8] in main
=========
```

### Root Cause

```cpp
int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
if (i > ACTIVE_CHANNELS * NUM_BINS)
    return; // out of range // histogram_gmem_atomics.h:88

unsigned int total = 0;
for (int j = 0; j < n; j++)
    total += in[i + NUM_PARTS * j];

out[i] = total; // histogram_gmem_atomics.h:95
```

The guard condition `if (i > ACTIVE_CHANNELS * NUM_BINS)` allows `i == ACTIVE_CHANNELS * NUM_BINS` (256 when `ACTIVE_CHANNELS=1`, `NUM_BINS=256`), but valid indices are `[0, 255]`. 

### Suggested Fix

```diff
--- a/src/histogram-cuda/histogram_gmem_atomics.h
+++ b/src/histogram-cuda/histogram_gmem_atomics.h
@@ -85,7 +85,7 @@
         unsigned int *out)
     {
         int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
-        if (i > ACTIVE_CHANNELS * NUM_BINS)
+        if (i >= ACTIVE_CHANNELS * NUM_BINS)
             return; // out of range

         unsigned int total = 0;
```

title: [BUG] Shared memory race conditions in `rsmt-cuda` at (`buildMST` and `insertSteinerPoints` kernels)
author: Mingkai-Li

title: [BUG] Shared memory race conditions in `sssp-cuda` at (`SSSP_gpu` kernel)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`sssp-cuda`: a race condition occurs in the `sssp-cuda` program's `SSSP_gpu` kernel.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/sssp-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
compute-sanitizer --tool=racecheck ./main -t 1
```

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: f6a9935b5327920d63c917163c75d5d847b6623b

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
========= Error: Race reported between Read access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x140 in main.cu:205
=========     and Write access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x1430 in main.cu:261 [12 hazards]
=========
========= Error: Race reported between Write access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x1500 in main.cu:268
=========     and Read access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x1680 in main.cu:273 [4 hazards]
```

### Root Cause and Suggested Fix

Unclear. When I tried to fix the race condition, the `compute-sanitizer`'s `racecheck` tool continue to report different race conditions. This is the patch I tried:

```diff
@@ -202,6 +202,7 @@ __global__ void SSSP_gpu(
   __syncthreads();

   int my_base = *base;
+  __syncthreads();
   while(my_base < n_t_local) {

     // If local queue might overflow
@@ -261,6 +262,7 @@ __global__ void SSSP_gpu(
       *base = atomicAdd(&head[0], WG_SIZE); // Fetch more frontier elements from the queue
     __syncthreads();
     my_base = *base;
+    __syncthreads();
   }
```

However, the `compute-sanitizer`'s `racecheck` tool continues to report different race conditions after applying the patch above:

```
========= Error: Race reported between Read access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x1a0 in main.cu:209
=========     and Write access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x390 in main.cu:224 [4 hazards]
=========
========= Error: Race reported between Read access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x1930 in main.cu:276
=========     and Write access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x770 in main.cu:255 [32 hazards]
=========     and Write access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x980 in main.cu:255 [32 hazards]

=========     and Read access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x1470 in main.cu:264 [4 hazards]
=========

=========     and Read access at SSSP_gpu(const Node *, const Edge *, int *, int *, const int *, int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, const int *, int *)+0x1930 in main.cu:276 [68 hazards]
```

title: [BUG] Heap buffer overflow in `floydwarshall2-cuda` at (`init2` kernel)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`floydwarshall2-cuda`: running the `floydwarshall2-cuda` program with the crafted input file `floydwarshall2-memcheck.txt` will cause a heap buffer overflow in the `init2` kernel.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/floydwarshall2-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
compute-sanitizer --tool=memcheck ./main ./floydwarshall2-memcheck.txt 1
```

### PoC

[floydwarshall2-memcheck.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29069541/floydwarshall2-memcheck.txt)

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: f6a9935b5327920d63c917163c75d5d847b6623b

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
========= Invalid __global__ read of size 4 bytes
=========     at init2(ECLgraph, int *, int)+0x160 in main.cu:73
=========     by thread (64,0,0) in block (0,0,0)
=========     Access to 0xd8133bffffc is out of bounds
=========     and is 4 bytes before the nearest allocation at 0xd8133c00000 of size 264 bytes
=========     Saved host backtrace up to driver entry point at kernel launch time
=========         Host Frame: cudaLaunchKernel [0x7c5d7] in main
=========         Host Frame: FW_gpu_64(ECLgraph, int*, int) [0x9b76] in main
=========         Host Frame: main [0x8dbf] in main
```

### Root Cause

```cpp
  if (i < g.nodes) {
    for (int j = g.nindex[i]; j < g.nindex[i + 1]; j++) {
      const int nei = g.nlist[j]; // main.cu:73
      AdjMat[i * upper + nei] = g.eweight[j];
    }
  }
```

The value of `j` is not checked before using as an index into the `g.nlist` array (max length of `g.nlist` is `g.edges`).

### Suggested Fix

```diff
@@ -70,6 +71,7 @@ static __global__ void init2(
   const int i = threadIdx.x + blockIdx.x * blockDim.x;
   if (i < g.nodes) {
     for (int j = g.nindex[i]; j < g.nindex[i + 1]; j++) {
+      assert(j >= 0 && j < g.edges);
       const int nei = g.nlist[j];
       AdjMat[i * upper + nei] = g.eweight[j];
     }
```

title: [BUG] Shared memory race conditions in `floydwarshall2-cuda` at (`FWrem_64` kernel)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`floydwarshall2-cuda`: running the `floydwarshall2-cuda` program with the crafted input file `floydwarshall2-racecheck.txt` will cause a race condition in the `FWrem_64` kernel.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/floydwarshall2-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
compute-sanitizer --tool=racecheck ./main ./floydwarshall2-racecheck.txt 1
```

### PoC

[floydwarshall2-racecheck.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29069067/floydwarshall2-racecheck.txt)

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: f6a9935b5327920d63c917163c75d5d847b6623b

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
========= Error: Race reported between Read access at FWrem_64(int *, int, int *, int *, int, int)+0x5f0 in main.cu:382
=========     and Write access at FWrem_64(int *, int, int *, int *, int, int)+0x3700 in main.cu:408 [128 hazards]
=========
========= Error: Race reported between Read access at FWrem_64(int *, int, int *, int *, int, int)+0x600 in main.cu:383
=========     and Write access at FWrem_64(int *, int, int *, int *, int, int)+0x3710 in main.cu:409 [128 hazards]
=========
```

### Root Cause

```cpp
for (int k = 0; k < tile; k++) {
const mtype sk_a = s_kj[idx2_a]; // main.cu:382
const mtype sk_b = s_kj[idx2_b]; // main.cu:383

// ...

for (int k = 0; k < tile; k++) {
    if (warp_a == k) s_kj[idx2_a] = ij_aa; // main.cu:408
    if (warp_a == k) s_kj[idx2_b] = ij_ab; // main.cu:409

```

`s_kj` is a single 64×64 shared-memory tile that gets reused for two different purposes within the same kernel invocation, by the same thread block, with no barrier between the two uses.

### Suggested Fix

```diff
@@ -393,6 +393,7 @@ void FWrem_64(
     idx2_a += tile;
     idx2_b += tile;
   }
+  __syncthreads();

   if ((y == z) && (y == x + 1) && (x != subm1)) { // the diagonal in next iteration
     const int idx1_aa = lane_a * tile + warp_a;
```

title: [BUG] Heap buffer overflow in `gc-cuda` at (`init` kernel)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`gc-cuda`: running the `gc-cuda` program with the crafted input file `gc-memcheck-2.txt` will cause a heap buffer overflow in the `init` kernel.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/gc-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
compute-sanitizer --tool=memcheck ./main ./gc-memcheck-2.txt 1
```

### PoC

[gc-memcheck-2.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29067880/gc-memcheck-2.txt)

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: f6a9935b5327920d63c917163c75d5d847b6623b

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
========= Invalid __global__ read of size 4 bytes
=========     at init(int, int, const int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, int *, int *, int *)+0xb70 in main.cu:98
=========     by thread (0,0,0) in block (0,0,0)
=========     Access to 0x64723bffff4 is out of bounds
=========     and is 12 bytes before the nearest allocation at 0x64723c00000 of size 264 bytes
=========     Saved host backtrace up to driver entry point at kernel launch time
=========         Host Frame: main [0x9315] in main
```

### Root Cause

```cpp
    active = 0;
    pos = beg;
    for (int i = beg; i < end; i++) {
        const int nei = nlist[i];
        const int degn = nidx[nei + 1] - nidx[nei]; // main.cu:98
```

The value of `nei` is not checked before using as an index into the `nidx` array (max length of `nidx` is `nodes`).

### Suggested Fix

```diff
 static const int ThreadsPerBlock = 512;
 static const unsigned int Warp = 0xffffffff;
@@ -95,6 +96,7 @@ void init(const int nodes,
         pos = beg;
         for (int i = beg; i < end; i++) {
           const int nei = nlist[i];
+          assert(nei >= 0 && (nei+1) < nodes);
           const int degn = nidx[nei + 1] - nidx[nei];
           if ((degv < degn) || ((degv == degn) && (hash(v) < hash(nei))) || ((degv == degn) && (hash(v) == hash(nei)) && (v < nei))) {
             active |= (unsigned int)MSB >> (i - beg);
```

title: [BUG] Heap buffer overflow in `gc-cuda` at (`init` kernel)
author: Mingkai-Li
body:
### Description

`gc-cuda`: running the `gc-cuda` program with the crafted input file `gc-memcheck-1.txt` will cause a heap buffer overflow in the `init` kernel.

### Reproduce

```bash
cd HeCBench/src/gc-cuda
make clean && make ARCH=sm_80 EXTRA_CFLAGS="-lineinfo"
compute-sanitizer --tool=memcheck ./main ./gc-memcheck-1.txt 1
```

### PoC

[gc-memcheck-1.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29067230/gc-memcheck-1.txt)

### Environment

- **CUDA version**: CUDA 13.2
- **GPU**: NVIDIA A30 (sm_80)
- **OS**: Ubuntu 24.04
- **Commit**: f6a9935b5327920d63c917163c75d5d847b6623b

### Relevant Log Snippet

```
========= Invalid __global__ read of size 4 bytes
=========     at init(int, int, const int *, const int *, int *, int *, int *, int *, int *, int *)+0x1440 in main.cu:120
=========     by thread (0,0,0) in block (0,0,0)
=========     Access to 0x83687c0ff94 is out of bounds
=========     and is 108 bytes before the nearest allocation at 0x83687c10000 of size 982528 bytes
=========     Saved host backtrace up to driver entry point at kernel launch time
=========         Host Frame: main [0x9315] in main
```


### Root Cause

```cpp
for (int i = wbeg + lane; __any_sync(Warp, i < wend); i += WS) {
        int wnei;
        bool prio = false;
        if (i < wend) {
          wnei = nlist[i]; // main.cu:120
```

The value of `i` is not checked before using as an index into the `nlist` array (max length of `nlist` is `edges`).

### Suggested Fix

```diff
 static const int ThreadsPerBlock = 512;
 static const unsigned int Warp = 0xffffffff;
@@ -117,6 +118,7 @@ void init(const int nodes,
         int wnei;
         bool prio = false;
         if (i < wend) {
+          assert (i >= 0 && i < edges);
           wnei = nlist[i];
           const int wdegn = nidx[wnei + 1] - nidx[wnei];
           prio = ((wdegv < wdegn) || ((wdegv == wdegn) && (hash(wv) < hash(wnei))) || ((wdegv == wdegn) && (hash(wv) == hash(wnei)) && (wv < wnei)));
```
